papers.title: A population of red candidate massive galaxies 600 Myr after the Big Bang
papers.abstract: JWST CEERS imaging reveals candidate high-redshift galaxies with inferred large stellar masses at early cosmic times. The analysis discusses photometric-redshift and stellar-population-modeling uncertainties that affect inferred mass and redshift.

papers.title: Early results from GLASS-JWST. A census of luminous galaxies at z > 7 from NIRCam
papers.abstract: GLASS-JWST NIRCam observations provide a census of luminous high-redshift galaxy candidates. The study highlights sensitivity to selection functions, completeness, and photometric-redshift uncertainties.

papers.title: UNCOVER: A NIRSpec survey of galaxies at z > 8 in the Abell 2744 field
papers.abstract: NIRSpec follow-up in the UNCOVER program provides spectroscopic constraints on galaxies at very high redshift. The paper discusses line-identification uncertainties and lensing-model dependence in inferred source properties.

papers.title: CEERS spectroscopy of massive galaxy candidates at high redshift with JWST/NIRSpec
papers.abstract: JWST/NIRSpec spectroscopy of CEERS high-redshift massive-galaxy candidates tests photometric inferences and refines stellar-mass estimates. The analysis emphasizes systematics from line contamination and SED-model assumptions.

papers.title: The stellar mass density at z > 8 from JWST surveys: implications for early galaxy assembly
papers.abstract: A synthesis of early JWST survey constraints on stellar mass density at high redshift examines consistency across CEERS, JADES, and GLASS-style selections. The paper discusses cosmic variance, selection effects, and model dependence.

extracted_text_by_key.2207.12446: CEERS candidates suggest unexpectedly large stellar masses at early times, but conclusions depend on photometric-redshift robustness, SED assumptions, and dust modeling.
extracted_text_by_key.2207.09436: GLASS-JWST high-redshift candidates constrain luminous galaxy abundance; completeness and photometric-redshift systematics remain central.
extracted_text_by_key.UNCOVER: A NIRSpec survey of galaxies at: NIRSpec spectroscopy confirms subsets of z>8 candidates while highlighting line-identification and lensing-model uncertainties.
extracted_text_by_key.CEERS spectroscopy of massive galaxy can: Spectroscopic follow-up revises some stellar-mass estimates and assesses contamination/systematic error sources in early massive-galaxy claims.
extracted_text_by_key.The stellar mass density at z > 8 from J: Cross-survey comparison indicates rapid early assembly remains plausible, with quantitative interpretation sensitive to cosmic variance and selection biases.
